summary: High‑energy Rochester show with monster High Time and scorching Let It Grow
blurb: The set bursts with a blistering High Time and a searing Let It Grow that rolls into an incendiary Midnight Hour encore. Brent’s slashing keyboards and the dual‑drum attack drive the second half, while Jerry’s guitar roars on Loser and Stella Blue. The performance is a standout example of late‑‘80s Dead fire.
review: On November 7, 1985 the Dead turned the Community War Memorial into a furnace of pure rock ’n’ roll. From the opening “Dancin’ In The Street” through a rollicking first set that builds to a monster Deal, the band kept the groove tight and the crowd moving. The second half is where the night truly ignites – “Touch of Grey” slides straight into a blistering “Samson & Delilah,” then erupts with a “High Time” that reviewers called “monster,” followed by a scorching “Let It Grow” that rolls right into a thunderous drum break and an explosive “In The Midnight Hour” encore. Jerry’s axe was “barking out the Loser” and delivered cool runs on “Stella Blue,” while Phil’s bass underpinned the high‑energy groove and even added a vocal shout‑out. Brent Mydland’s keyboards were described as “slashing” and “Brent rocks!” adding fire to every jam, and the dual‑drum assault from Mickey and Bill (with Bill grabbing the mic for crowd claps) provided the relentless drive that made this show a late‑‘80s highlight.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across the majority of recordings: descriptors like “monster,” “blistering,” “scorching,” and “high‑energy” appear in multiple SBD reviews; no consistent negative comments about playing, only a few mixed notes on unknown sources. This consensus adds a substantial boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard remix with crystal‑clear mix, superb balance and no dropouts; captures the monster High Time, scorching Let It Grow and the fiery Touch > Samson sequence perfectly.

key_highlights: Monster High Time and scorching Let It Grow ignite the second set
"Touch of Grey" into "Samson & Delilah" delivers a high‑octane sequence
Fiery Midnight Hour encore burns with intensity
Brent’s slashing keyboards and dual‑drum power drive the night
song_highlights: Beat It On Down The Line

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is on fire – “barking out the Loser,” delivering cool runs on Stella Blue and driving the high‑energy momentum throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Phil provides solid bass foundation and notable vocal contributions, anchoring the groove especially during the high‑octane second set.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s keyboards are described as “slashing” and “rocking,” adding fire to every jam and sharing vocals on the Midnight Hour encore.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming intensifies with the set, especially during Let It Grow and the second‑set climaxes.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann adds extra power with the dual‑drum kit, grabs the mic to lead crowd clapping, and contributes to highlighted drum solo breakouts.

must_listen_sequences: Beat It On Down The Line
